codeReview.summary: Core onboarding flow and template generation are implemented and covered by tests, but the feature introduces a blocking global-configuration bug and one input-validation gap.

issues.description: Onboarding persists a hardcoded global CORS setting (`cors.allowed-origins = http://localhost:3002`) when the key is missing. Because `SystemSettingsService` prioritizes persisted values (`SystemSettingsService.java:68-70`), this can override environment-configured production origins after the first onboarding and break/incorrectly constrain real frontend access.

`timezone` only enforces non-blank/length, but onboarding uses it directly (`TenantOnboardingService.java:116`) and then calls `CompanyTime.today` (`TenantOnboardingService.java:97`), which resolves via `ZoneId.of` (`CompanyClock.java:66`). Invalid timezone strings therefore surface as runtime failures instead of a clean validation error response.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature writes global `system_settings` directly inside a tenant onboarding service instead of following the centralized settings flow. This creates drift risk versus environment-driven defaults.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: TenantOnboardingService.java:224-236 writes `auto-approval.enabled`, `period-lock.enforced`, and `cors.allowed-origins` directly; SystemSettingsService.java:35-70 is the existing centralized runtime/settings-loading path.

summary: Reviewed commit 6c068cc2646ef848e6d73e19b50b33dc56bfb8ae for tenant onboarding templates. Feature scope is largely met (template API, single-call onboarding, account hierarchy generation, default period, tests, frontend handoff), but review fails due to a blocking hardcoded global CORS persistence bug; also noted non-blocking timezone validation weakness.
